domains.name: Persistence
domains.purpose: PII redaction, Redis Streams, atomic file writes

domains.name: Config
domains.purpose: Signal resolution, runtime overrides, audit logging, runtime JSON configs

domains.name: Observability
domains.purpose: Prometheus metrics for signals, tiers, enforcement, entitlements

domains.name: Runtime
domains.purpose: Decision engine, adaptive baseline, causal analysis, deployment safety loop

domains.name: Risk
domains.purpose: Sliding-window risk scoring, risk band classification, warning store

domains.name: Tiers
domains.purpose: Tier registry, tier-to-signal mapping, wildcard pattern matching

domains.name: Entitlements
domains.purpose: Tier entitlements registry, enforcement middleware, concurrency limiter, retention enforcement

domains.name: Evidence
domains.purpose: Evidence envelope generation, canonicalization, deterministic JSON output

domains.name: API
domains.purpose: HTTP ingest, evidence export, pilot dashboard, health endpoints

domains.name: CLI
domains.purpose: Binary entry points: consumer, decision-engine, signalctl, test-runner, generators

domains.name: Test Harness
domains.purpose: Anomaly detection testing, merchant simulation, telemetry analysis

domains.name: Infrastructure
domains.purpose: Docker, docker-compose, k8s, systemd, Vector, deployment scripts

primitives.name: RiskScorer
primitives.responsibility: Sliding-window per-processor risk scoring with weighted multi-component model
inputs: Event (event_type, processor, failure_origin, retry_count, geo_bucket, result)
primitives.notes: Same event sequence over same window produces identical score. Fixed-size bucket array per processor, score clamped to [0, 1].

primitives.name: WarningStore
primitives.responsibility: LRU-bounded in-memory storage of risk warnings with outcome annotation
inputs: Warning (risk score, band, drivers, processor, merchant, event metadata)
primitives.notes: LRU eviction is ordered, outcome annotation is idempotent. Fixed capacity, oldest entries evicted.

primitives.name: AdaptiveBaseline
primitives.responsibility: Exponential-smoothing baseline learning for error rate, P95 latency, and memory
outputs: snapshot (means and variances for all metrics)
primitives.notes: deviationScore() and z() are pure functions. Update() is deterministic given identical input order, but output depends on accumulated state history. Replay of the same metric sequence from the same initial snapshot produces identical results. Sample count capped at 100,000.

primitives.name: TierRegistry
primitives.responsibility: Precompiled tier-to-signal mapping with O(1) exact-match and O(n) wildcard lookup
inputs: TierConfig (JSON mapping of tier to signal patterns)
primitives.notes: Compiled from static JSON config, atomic.Value for lock-free reads. Four tiers (baseline, proof, shield, fortress), finite signal set.

primitives.name: EntitlementsRegistry
primitives.responsibility: Tier-specific capability and limit management with fail-closed defaults
inputs: EntitlementsConfig (JSON mapping of tier to entitlement values)
primitives.notes: O(1) lookup via atomic.Value, fail-closed on unknown tier. Four tiers, validated ranges for all fields.

primitives.name: OverrideStore
primitives.responsibility: Thread-safe copy-on-write storage for runtime signal overrides
primitives.notes: Copy-on-write semantics, atomic.Value for lock-free reads. Bounded by signal count, overrides expire via TTL.

primitives.name: GenerateEnvelope
primitives.responsibility: Deterministic evidence assembly: Filter → Normalize → Stabilize → Sort → Clamp → Canonicalize
primitives.notes: Key sorting, severity coercion, forbidden key stripping, deterministic JSON. Output clamped, artifacts filtered by retention.

primitives.name: ConcurrencyLimiter
primitives.notes: Atomic int64 increment/decrement. Limited by max_concurrent_requests per tier.

primitives.name: TraceLog
primitives.responsibility: Fixed-size ring buffer for Decision Engine evaluation traces, supporting deterministic replay
inputs: Trace (timestamp, metrics, baseline, score, decision, invariant)
primitives.notes: Ring buffer index is sequential, snapshot ordering is deterministic. Default size 500, max load size 10 MB.

primitives.name: Timeline
primitives.responsibility: Bounded ring-buffer for decision history entries
primitives.notes: Ring buffer append is sequential. Default size 300.

primitives.name: logsafe
primitives.responsibility: PII redaction via denylist of sensitive keys and allowlist of safe keys
primitives.notes: Denylist/allowlist are static. Recursive traversal depth bounded by input structure.

primitives.name: ratelimit.Limiter
primitives.responsibility: Atomic token-bucket rate limiting via Redis Lua script
primitives.notes: Given identical (now, state, config) tuple, the Lua script produces identical output. Non-determinism arises only from wall-clock reads and concurrent Redis state.

primitives.name: DecisionEngine
primitives.responsibility: Top-level orchestrator: collects metrics, computes deviation score, evaluates thresholds, emits Decision, coordinates baseline learning, invariant checking, causal analysis, timeline, and trace persistence
inputs: Config (MetricsURL, HealthURL, OutputFile, BaselinePath, TimelinePath, TracePath, Interval)
outputs: Decision (status, confidence, reason, cause, timestamp, deploy_age_sec, version)
primitives.notes: evaluate() is a pure function of (score, age); the full loop depends on external HTTP metrics and wall clock. Single-instance via flock, fixed evaluation interval, bounded sub-components.

primitives.name: InvariantValidator
primitives.responsibility: Pure-function validation that Decision Engine outputs satisfy mathematical and logical invariants
primitives.notes: Same inputs produce same report. O(1), no IO, no time.Now(), no randomness. Fixed set of 7 invariant checks.

primitives.name: CausalAnalyzer
primitives.responsibility: Root-cause attribution via z-score comparison of live metrics against learned baseline, weighted by deploy age
primitives.notes: Pure function of inputs. Cause enum is closed (traffic_spike, memory_leak, slow_dependency, cold_start, deploy_impact, unknown). Confidence clamped to [0, 1].

primitives.name: MetricsCollector
primitives.responsibility: HTTP scraping of Prometheus exposition metrics and health endpoint; parses error rate, P95 latency, and memory usage
primitives.notes: Depends on external HTTP responses. 5s HTTP timeout, fixed metric key set.

artifacts.name: Decision
artifacts.schema: {status, confidence, reason, cause, timestamp, deploy_age_sec, version}

artifacts.name: Warning
artifacts.schema: {warning_id, event_id, processor, merchant_id_hash, risk_score, risk_band, risk_drivers, outcome_*}

artifacts.name: Envelope
artifacts.schema: {schemaVersion, generatedAt, meta, payload{merchants, artifacts, narratives, system}}

artifacts.name: Baseline snapshot
artifacts.schema: {Samples, ErrMean, ErrVar, P95Mean, P95Var, MemMean, MemVar}

artifacts.name: AuditEntry
artifacts.schema: {timestamp, operator, signal_id, action, old_value?, new_value?, metadata?}

artifacts.name: PilotOutcomeAnnotation
artifacts.schema: {type, warning_id, processor, event_id, outcome_type, outcome_timestamp, outcome_source, outcome_notes?, lead_time_seconds?, annotated_at}

extension_rules.rule: New signals must be added to config/signals.runtime.json with deterministic flag, type, and enabled state, and registered in internal/specs/signal-registry.v1.json
New signals must be assigned to at least one tier in config/tiers.runtime.json
New tiers must be added to config/tier_entitlements.runtime.json and validated against JSON Schema with monotonic tier progression
New signal types must work with the existing OverrideStore resolution chain (tier check → runtime override → canonical spec)
Override mutations must flow through SetOverrideWithAudit or DeleteOverrideWithAudit to produce an AuditEntry
New runtime buffers or stores must declare a fixed capacity; unbounded growth is prohibited
Files written by runtime processes must use the tmp + fsync + rename pattern
Each signal must declare deterministic: true or deterministic: false
No external ML dependencies; scoring must use arithmetic on counters and fixed weights
Runtime decisions (Decision Engine, risk scoring) are advisory; no auto-execution of external actions
Payloads logged or persisted must pass through logsafe.RedactJSON or logsafe.SafeFieldsFromWebhook
New subsystems must expose relevant Prometheus counters, gauges, or histograms with payflux_ prefix
